summary: Group favourites (FIFA 7th) and Euro 2024 semi-finalists under Ronald Koeman — a technical, short-passing side, but pragmatic rather than the free-flowing Dutch of old. Effective but at times sterile and predictable, lacking the fluid rotations that once defined them.
style: Base 4-2-3-1 that becomes a 3-2-5 once settled in the opposition half — one full-back (usually Dumfries) pushes high while the other tucks in to form a back three. Builds down one flank to drag the opponent across, then switches to the free winger/full-back. Generates overloads to free 1v1 dribblers (Gakpo, Malen). Heavily reliant on the right side: 40% of their attacking touches came down Dumfries' flank at Euro 2024.

counterReliance.note: Pragmatic possession; hard to play through, not a counter side

strengths: Possession control with 1v1 dribblers and overloads to switch into space
Denzel Dumfries as a relentless right-sided attacking outlet
Van Dijk's authority and long diagonals to inject urgency
Strong defensively — conceded just once from set pieces in qualifying
weaknesses: Can be slow and predictable in attack ('perhaps too slow' — Van Dijk)
No recognised target man (top scorer Depay is really a wide forward) — long balls rarely viable
Forwards aren't natural pressers — they sit and stay compact rather than win it high

keyPlayers.name: Frenkie de Jong
keyPlayers.note: The beating heart — superb passing range and ball-carrying to escape pressure; his Euro 2024 absence was keenly felt

keyPlayers.name: Virgil van Dijk
keyPlayers.note: Authority and assurance — dribbled past just 35 times across his last eight Premier League seasons

keyPlayers.name: Cody Gakpo
keyPlayers.note: The 1v1 threat the overloads are built to free — goals and assists from the left

tactics: Control possession, overload one side and switch to free a dribbler, with Dumfries the key right-sided outlet; defend by being hard to play through rather than pressing high. Koeman is reportedly weighing specialist set-piece coaching to add a missing cutting edge from dead balls.
notes: Opening fixture vs Japan is a clash of styles — Dutch control vs Japanese high-octane transition.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group F (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
